stance: Quality-growth core, modestly de-risked: lean into rising-revision compounders, prune falling-revision laggards, add liquid value-with-revisions large/mids.
experience_note: This is an early round with no graded track record yet (last_round_review is null), so weights are pre-registered, not hindsight-fit. What I am watching: whether my rising-revision adds (ONGC, LICI, Muthoot, Coforge, Grasim, Gland) outperform the falling-revision names I exited (ADANIPORTS, LT, BIOCON, AMBUJACEM, MFSL) over the next 1-3 months — that is the direct test of the analyst-revision tilt. What would change my mind: if momentum z-scores turn sharply positive somewhere (a real trend emerges) I would re-weight toward it; if my rising-revision adds underperform the exits, the revision tilt is not transferring and I cut my reliance on it next round. I am also watching that trimming beta does not simply give up upside in a rally — if the book lags the benchmark in an up-tape, the de-risking was too aggressive.
